description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with stages pre-build, build, staging, and deploy and the variables CONTAINER_DEV_IMAGE, DEPLOY_HOST, DOCKER_DRIVER, GRADLE_OPTS. In the pre-build stage add a build_test job using the adoptopenjdk/openjdk8 image that installs apt-transport-https, ca-certificates, docker.io, wget, unzip, openssh-client, prepares SSH keys, runs wget -nv -O ~/gradle-4.7-bin.zip https://services.gradle.org/distributions/gradle-4.7-bin.zip, unzip -q -d /opt/gradle/ ~/gradle-4.7-bin.zip, /opt/gradle/gradle-4.7/bin/gradle --version, /opt/gradle/gradle-4.7/bin/gradle wrapper, adds /opt/gradle/gradle-4.7/bin to PATH, writes the PATH export to /etc/profile.d/gradle.sh, runs ./gradlew build --no-daemon, and stores dist/trainSchedule.zip as an artifact. In the build stage add a build_docker job using the docker:git image with docker:dind service that creates ~/.ssh, disables StrictHostKeyChecking, writes $SSH_PRIVATE_KEY to id_rsa, builds the image train‑schedule‑image with docker build -t train-schedule-image ., lists images, stops and removes any existing container on $DEPLOY_HOST via SSH, removes the old image, prunes Docker volumes, streams the image with docker save | ssh ... sudo docker load, verifies the image on the host, logs into the GitLab registry with docker login -u gitlab-ci-token -p "$CI_BUILD_TOKEN" "$CI_REGISTRY", builds $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:$CI_COMMIT_SHA and pushes it. In the deploy stage add a deploy_docker job (only on master) that installs openssh-client if needed, sets up ~/.ssh and ~/.docker, writes $SSH_PRIVATE_KEY and $DOCKER_AUTH_CONFIG, runs ssh to print hostname, runs sudo docker run --restart always --name train-schedule -p 8080:8080 -d train-schedule-image on the remote host, sleeps, and checks the container with ssh and docker ps. Also add deploy_prd_K8s and deploy_stg_K8s jobs (only on master) that use docker:git with docker:dind, install kubectl via apk and curl, configure the Kubernetes cluster (do2 for production, do1 for staging) with kubectl config set-cluster, set-credentials, set-context, use-context, create a docker‑registry secret, substitute _APP_NAME_ and _VERSION_ in kubernetes.tpl.yml to produce kubernetes.yml, and apply it to the production or staging namespace, with the production job marked manual.
